compiled_state.title: record-review.sh: review-status format mismatch — writes pass:hash but review gate expects multi-line 'passed'
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
When record-review.sh is called manually (outside of the full review workflow), it may write review-status in a single-line format (pass:hash) instead of the multi-line format (passed on line 1, diff_hash=hash on line 2) that pre-commit-review-gate.sh expects. The gate checks head -1 of review-status against the literal string 'passed' — any other format is rejected.

## Expected Behavior
record-review.sh should always write review-status in the format expected by pre-commit-review-gate.sh (multi-line: 'passed' on line 1, 'diff_hash=<hash>' on subsequent line).

## Actual Behavior
When invoked improperly (e.g., orchestrator writes pass:hash directly), the review gate blocks with 'Review status is pass:hash (must be passed)'. The record-review.sh script does write the correct format when invoked with --reviewer-hash, but the error messaging could be clearer about the required invocation.
